label: Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, LD style — front band: living (left) + dining (middle) + standard bedroom (right). Rear band: master + ensuite (left), hall + common T&B stacked (hall in front, T&B behind it) in the middle, kitchen (right). Hall is the circulation hinge for the T&B and kitchen: open to kitchen and to dining in front of it, and hosts the common T&B's door. Master doors into living (its south wall, a corner swing), not the hall. Neither a clean vertical split nor a horizontal split — private and public occupy diagonal corners instead.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — carved from master's own footprint via the alcove claim. Door from master only, no other access.
ensuite and common bath (T&B) share a plumbing wall, consolidating both wet stacks in the rear-middle band. No door — ensuite stays master-exclusive per the locked catalog convention.

master's east wall = hall's west wall — geometry-only wall now, NO door (was master's door before 2026-07-22). The master<->hall shared segment sits mid-wall on master's much-wider east wall, so a door there can't swing to a corner (no perpendicular wall on master's side); master's door moved to living instead, which always touches master's south-wall corners. Master still reaches the hall visually — hall is open to dining, which master opens onto.

master's door — opens south into living, hinged at master's SE corner (door_placement=high_corner) so the panel swings flat against the EAST wall (solid vs dining below y=5): a corner swing at the SE corner, next to the dining/hall junction. Living is directly in front of master (via the [living,master] stack) and ALWAYS shares master's full south wall with real corners at every lot size, unlike master<->dining/hall on the east wall, which only touch at some sizes and can't give a robust SE-corner swing (at compact lots the hall widens between master and dining). Replaced the old master<->hall door (2026-07-22). Living is public (valid bedroom access per the validator's ACCESS_FROM set), and it's one continuous open LDK with dining/kitchen anyway.
T&B's door opens into the hall — matches the catalog's standard 'common bath is hall/public-accessible, not routed through a bedroom' rule.

hall's front wall is an open mouth into dining — codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan, no door. Dining is the middle public room, directly in front of the hall (it took over the mouth role the merged great_room used to play). This is hall's (and by extension master's and common's) connection to the public wing.

kitchen's west wall opens onto the hall (open-plan, no door) — hall serves as kitchen's entrance from the private/circulation side. Kitchen is NOT adjacent to dining or living: it reaches the public wing only via the hall. Keeping kitchen and dining non-adjacent is deliberate — it avoids the 3-way mutual adjacency (hall/kitchen/dining) that made the pre-2026-07-14 living+dining version infeasible.

standard's rear wall = kitchen's front wall — geometry-only, NO door. Keeps the building outline a clean rectangle at this seam, but deliberately does not give a bedroom a direct opening onto the kitchen work area; standard's actual door is to dining instead.

standard's only door — direct into dining (the front-middle public room, immediately west of standard). 1.1 m floor = 0.8 m clear panel + frame + a little leeway. Took over the door role standard<->great_room played before the split.
living and dining sit side by side across the front (living left, dining middle) — open plan, no wall. Side-by-side (not stacked front-to-rear) requires ldk_horizontal: true.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: The source concept sketch draws ensuite very small (~1.5 x 1.5 m, ~2.25 sqm) — tighter than every other ensuite in the catalog (which float around 2.5-3.0 sqm). auto_apply floors it at 2.2 sqm to stay close to the drawn concept while keeping it just above the 1.2 sqm hard minimum used elsewhere; preferred_apply lets it grow to 2.5 sqm where the lot allows. Kitchen and common bath floors match the rest of the wide-shell catalog.

setback_elements.note: kitchen sits in the rear band in this topology, so the standard rear_setback dirty-kitchen pattern applies normally.

notes: Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, LD style.
2026-07-21: great_room split back into separate LIVING + DINING rooms —
finally matching this topology's '_ld' name (it had shipped a great_room
since the 2026-07-14 revision, a name/implementation mismatch). The
original living+dining version (pre-2026-07-14) was infeasible even at
34x34 m; two things unlock the split now: (1) ldk_horizontal: true
disables the solver's hardcoded LDK vertical-stacking rules (living must
sit in front of dining+kitchen), which contradicted the side-by-side
living/dining across the front; (2) kitchen is kept NON-adjacent to
dining (reached only via the hall, as the great_room version already
did), avoiding the 3-way mutual adjacency (hall/kitchen/dining) that was
the original blocker. Verified 0-warning across ~13x10 to 16x12; the
oversized-envelope failure (25x18+) is now just the ordinary
non-monotonic upper bound, not the total infeasibility the original hit.
claim_dead_strips: true absorbs the interior pockets that ragged widths
leave at the larger end of the band into L-alcoves.
Layout intent (canonical form, street/front at the bottom):
Front band (bottom): [living (hosts entry) | dining | standard ]
Rear band  (top):    [master + ensuite | hall / common T&B (stacked) | kitchen ]
living spans the front-left (in front of master); dining spans the
front-middle (in front of the hall/common column); standard spans the
front-right (in front of kitchen only). living + dining are open-plan
to each other across the front.
Circulation: hall is the hinge between the T&B, the kitchen, and the
public wing. dining -> hall (open mouth, no door) -> common T&B (door).
hall also opens directly onto kitchen (open-plan) — kitchen's only route
to/from the rest of the house besides being visually open to it. kitchen
has no direct adjacency to living or dining; you reach it via hall. Master
does NOT door into the hall (2026-07-22 change) — its door is on its south
wall into living instead (a corner swing; see below).
standard's only door is to dining. standard<->kitchen is geometry-only
(wall, no door) — deliberately no direct bedroom-to-kitchen opening.
zone_split is null here (unlike the vertical- and horizontal-split
topologies) — the private/public boundary isn't a single axis, so
per-room 'zone' tags carry the classification instead.
Confirmed target_shell: wide — the sketch's ~10x7 m footprint (1.43:1)
matches the wide-shell catalog's proportions much more closely than
squarish.
Floor band: roughly 65-85 sqm buildable, sized against the sketch's ~70
sqm footprint (10 x 7 m building on a 14x11 buildable envelope).
No fallback declared.
